subject: Choose stack and scaffold app skeleton
description:
Select the web framework (Flask vs. FastAPI) and charting library, then scaffold a minimal Python web app that runs on localhost with a single command. Establish project structure, dependency management, and a basic served page. Bind to localhost only.

Selection criteria (per spec §7.2): minimal footprint, easy single-command run, charting library must support shaded range bands + multi-series overlays attractively.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

_Functional:_
- [ ] Framework and charting library chosen with rationale documented (resolves Open Questions #1)
- [ ] Project structure, dependency manifest, and entry point created
- [ ] `python app.py` (or equivalent single command) starts the server and serves a basic page on localhost
- [ ] Server binds to localhost only (not reachable from other machines)

_Edge Cases:_
- [ ] Port already in use surfaces a clear message rather than a stack trace

_Error Handling:_
- [ ] Missing dependencies produce an actionable error/instruction

**Testing Requirements:**
• Unit: App factory/startup returns a valid app instance
• Integration: Server responds 200 on the root route
• Smoke: Single-command launch renders the base page on localhost

Source: specs/fishy-SPEC.md Section 9.1 / 7.2
activeForm: Choosing stack and scaffolding app skeleton
